task_completion.description: A terminal window is open and shows command activity
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal/console window is visible on screen with text output

task_completion.description: Filter channel created via REST API
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows curl POST to /api/channels with XML body, and a channel ID returned

task_completion.description: Channel has filter logic for ADT message type
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows channel XML with filter rules, JavaScript filter code referencing MSH-9 or ADT, or RuleBuilder elements
Terminal shows deployment command executed or dashboard shows channel in STARTED state

task_completion.description: Filter was tested with ADT and non-ADT messages
task_completion.visual_evidence: Terminal shows message send commands for both ADT and ORU messages, with different outcomes (ADT passes, ORU filtered)

integrity.visual_evidence: Channel XML or API response shows port 6662 configured

privileged_info_for_vlm: The task requires creating a channel with a filter that only accepts ADT messages. Success indicators: (1) channel created with filter rules referencing MSH-9 message type, (2) channel deployed on port 6662, (3) ADT messages pass through while ORU messages are filtered out.
